null;\\n  }\\n\\n  /** Values for HUD meter ids (d1, d2, rpe, hunger, coupling, habit). */\\n  public static getMeterValues(): Record<string, number> {\\n    const snap = this.getSnapshot();\\n    return {\\n      d1: snap.d1,\\n      d2: snap.d2,\\n      rpe: snap.rpe,\\n      hunger: snap.drugHunger,\\n      coupling: snap.insulaAccCoupling,\\n      habit: snap.habitEncoding\\n    };\\n (this.d2 < REGULATION.lowD2Threshold) {\\n      jumpDelayMultiplier = THRESHOLDS.lowD2JumpDelayFactor;\\n    }\\n    return { speedMultiplier, jumpDelayMultiplier };\\n  }\\n\\n  public static /** Maps config `volumeZone` strings (e.g. safe, accAnchor) to internal zone flags. */\\n  public static setVolumeZone(zoneId: string, active: boolean): void {\\n    if (zoneId === 'safe' || zoneId     break;\\n      case 'safeZone':\\n        this.inSafeZone = true;\\n        break;\\n      case 'accAnchor':\\n        this.inAccAnchor = true;\\n        this.insulaAccCoupling = this.clamp01(\\n    default:\\n        break;\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  public static applyDrugUse(): void {\\n    const drug = this.config.DRUG;\\n    this.d1 = this.clamp01(this.d1 + drug.d1UpregPerUse);\\n    this.d2 = this.clamp01(this.d2 - drug.d2DownregPerUse);\\n    this.drugHunger = this.clamp01(this.drugHunger - drug.hungerRelief);\\n    this.lastDrugUseMs = Date.now();\\n {\\n    const cue = this.config.CUE;\\n    this.fireRPE(cue.actualReward, magnitude);\\n  }\\n\\n  public static adjustState(target: StateAdjustTarget, amount: number): void {\\n    switch (target) 'drugHunger':\\n        this.drugHunger = this.clamp01(this.drugHunger + amount);\\n        break;\\n      case 'habitEncoding':\\n        this.habitEncoding = this.clamp01(this.habitEncoding + private static applyPulse(kind: StatePulseKind, magnitude: number): void {\\n    if (kind === 'drug') {\\n      this.applyDrugUse();\\n      return;\\n    }\\n    if (kind === 'craving') {\\n      this.applyCueRPE(magnitude);\\n      return;\\n    }\\n    this.fireRPE(magnitude, magnitude);\\n  }\\n\\n  private static fireRPE(actualReward: number, scale = 1): void {\\n    const delta = this.config.DECAY.habitGainFromRpe * this.rpe\\n    );\\n    this.checkLose();\\n  }\\n\\n  private static tick(dt: number): void {\\n    if (this.outcome !== 'playing') {\\n      return;\\n    }\\n\\n    const decay = this.config.DECAY;\\n    const reg = this.config.REGULATION;\\n\\n    const halfLife = decay.rpeHalfLifeMs / 1000;\\n    if (halfLife > 0) {\\n      const decayFactor = this.clamp01(\\n        this.insulaAccCoupling + reg.safeZoneCouplingBoost * dt\\n      );\\n    }\\n\\n    if (this.inAccAnchor) {\\n      this.insulaAccCoupling = this.clamp01(\\n        this.insulaAccCoupling + reg.accAnchorCouplingPerTick * dt\\n      );\\n    }\\n\\n    if (this.inAccAnchor && this.labelUrgeHeld) {\\n      this.accAwareness = this.clamp01(\\n        this.accAwareness + decay.accAwarenessRisePerSecondWhileLabeling * dt\\n      );\\n    } else {\\n      this.accAwareness = this.clamp01(this.accAwareness - decay.accAwarenessDecayPerSecond * dt);\\n    }\\n\\n    if (!this.inAccAnchor && !this.inSafeZone) {\\n      this.insulaAccCoupling = this.clamp01(\\n        this.insulaAccCoupling - decay.couplingDecayPerSecond * dt\\n      );\\n    }\\n\\n    this.maybeNotifyThresholds();\\n    this.checkWin(dt);\\n    this.checkLose();\\n  }\\n\\n  private static checkWin(dt: number): void {\\n    const win = this.config.LOSE_WIN;\\n    if (\\n (this.regulationSecondsAccum >= win.winRegulationSeconds) {\\n        this.outcome = 'won';\\n        this.showOutcome(\\n          'Regulation restored — goal-directed control returning',\\n          'rgba(0, 120, 80, 0.9)'\\n        );\\n      }\\n    } else {\\n      this.regulationSecondsAccum = 0;\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  private static checkLose(): void {\\n    if (this.habitEncoding >= this.config.LOSE_WIN.habitEncodingLose) {\\n      this.outcome = 'lost';\\n      this.showOutcome('Habit encoded — compulsive seeking dominates', 'rgba(140, 20, 40, 0.92)');\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  private static maybeNotifyThresholds(): void {\\n    if (!this.scene) {\\n      return;\\n    }\\n    const reg = this.config.REGULATION;\\n    if (this.d2 < reg.lowD2Threshold && !this.notifiedLowD2) {\\n      this.notifiedLowD2 = true;\\n      Notification.create({\\n        scene: this.scene,\\n        message: 'Brakes failing — OFC/ACC hypoactive (low D2)',\\n        duration: 3500,\\n        position: 'top-right',\\n        background: 'rgba(40, 40, 80, 0.88)',\\n        color: '#eef'\\n      });\\n    }\\n    if (this.d2 >= reg.lowD2Threshold + 0.08) {\\n      this.notifiedLowD2 = false;\\n    }\\n    if (\\n      this.drugHunger > reg.highHungerThreshold &&\\n      this.accAwareness < reg.lowAccThreshold &&\\n      !this.notifiedHighHunger\\n    ) {\\n      this.notifiedHighHunger = true;\\n      Notification.create({\\n        scene: this.scene,\\n        message: 'Drug hunger — insula signaling urges the ACC',\\n        duration: 3500,\\n        position: 'top-left',\\n        background: 'rgba(80, 30, 30, 0.88)',\\n        color: '#fdd'\\n      });\\n    }\\n    if (this.drugHunger < reg.highHungerThreshold - 0.1) {\\n      this.notifiedHighHunger = false;\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  private static showOutcome(message: string, background: string): void {\\n    if (!this.scene) {\\n      return;\\n    }\\n fontWeight: '600'\\n    });\\n  }\\n\\n  private static clamp01(value: number): number {\\n    return Math.max(0, Math.min(1, value));\\n  }\\n}\\n\",\"sync/configured_items_sync.ts\":\"// ============================================================================\\n// CONFIGURED ITEMS SYNC — replicate `environment.items` (collectibles + cake)\\n// ============================================================================\\n//\\n// Mirrors `environment_physics_sync.ts` but for items spawned by the\\n// `CollectiblesManager` (both collectibles such as Presents and non-collectible\\n// physics items such as Cake). Replication splits along the hybrid authority model\\n// defined in MULTIPLAYER_SYNCH.md:\\n//\\n//   - Non-collectible dynamic items (e.g. Cake) use **per-item authority**\\n//     (§4.7 / §7.5). Only the current item owner publishes transform rows.\\n//     (../../../MULTIPLAYER_SYNCH.md#47-item-authority-lifecycle)\\n//   - Collectibles (e.g. Presents) use **first-write-wins collection events**\\n//     (§5.2). Transform is bootstrap-only and MAY be emitted by the base\\n//     synchronizer. (../../../MULTIPLAYER_SYNCH.md#52-item-state)\\n//\\n// Today's implementation still samples from whichever client is the base\\n// synchronizer; the per-item-authority implementation plan layers the\\n// claim/release and owner-check logic on top of this module.\\n\\nimport { CollectiblesManager } from '../managers/collectibles_manager';\\nimport { sampleMeshPose } from '../utils/multiplayer_serialization';\\n\\nimport { ItemSync } from './item_sync';\\n\\nimport type { ItemCollectionEvent, ItemInstanceState } from '../types/multiplayer';\\n\\n/** Separator used when env-scoping a local CollectiblesManager instance id onto the wire. */\\nconst ENV_ITEM_SEPARATOR = '::';\\n\\nexport function envScopedInstanceId(environmentName: string, localId: string): string {\\n  return `${environmentName}${ENV_ITEM_SEPARATOR}${localId}`;\\n}\\n\\nexport function parseEnvScopedInstanceId(\\n  wireInstanceId: string\\n): { envName: string; localId: string } | null {\\n  const idx = wireInstanceId.indexOf(ENV_ITEM_SEPARATOR);\\n  if (idx <= 0) {\\n    return null;\\n  }\\n  const envName = wireInstanceId.slice(0, idx);\\n  const localId = wireInstanceId.slice(idx + ENV_ITEM_SEPARATOR.length);\\n  if (!envName || !localId) {\\n    return null;\\n  }\\n  return { envName, localId };\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Builds `ItemInstanceState` rows for every collectible + non-collectible physics item\\n * tracked by the CollectiblesManager. Each row carries the mesh's pose as `{ pos, rot }`\\n * per Invariants P and E in MULTIPLAYER_SYNCH.md §5.2; no separate rotation-Euler or\\n * velocity fields, and no matrix decomposition on the sample path. Env-scoped instance\\n * ids let peers filter by their current environment.\\n */\\nexport function sampleConfiguredItems(environmentName: string): ItemInstanceState[] {\\n  if (environmentName.trim() === '') {\\n    return [];\\n  }\\n\\n  const ts = Date.now();\\n  const out: ItemInstanceState[] = [];\\n\\n  const push = (\\n    id: string,\\n    entry: {\\n      mesh: BABYLON.AbstractMesh;\\n      body: BABYLON.PhysicsAggregate | null;\\n      config: { name: string };\\n    }\\n  ): void => {\\n    const mesh = entry.mesh;\\n    if (!mesh || mesh.isDisposed()) {\\n      return;\\n    }\\n    const isCollected = CollectiblesManager.isCollectedInstance(id);\\n    const pose = sampleMeshPose(mesh);\\n    out.push({\\n      instanceId: envScopedInstanceId(environmentName, id),\\n      itemName: entry.config.name,\\n      pos: pose.pos,\\n      rot: pose.rot,\\n      isCollected,\\n      timestamp: ts\\n    });\\n  };\\n\\n  for (const [id, entry] of CollectiblesManager.getCollectibleEntries()) {\\n    push(id, entry);\\n  }\\n  for (const [id, entry] of CollectiblesManager.getPhysicsItemEntries()) {\\n    push(id, entry);\\n  }\\n\\n  return out;\\n}\\n\\n/** Non-synchronizer apply: transform teleport + collection gating for configured items. */\\nexport function applyRemoteConfiguredItemState(\\n  currentEnvironmentName: string,\\n  state: ItemInstanceState\\n): void {\\n  const parsed = parseEnvScopedInstanceId(state.instanceId);\\n  if (!parsed || parsed.envName !== currentEnvironmentName) {\\n    // Silently drop rows for other environments. The snapshot is still retained in\\n    // the caller's `knownItemStates` map so it can be re-applied when the local\\n    // client switches into this env (MULTIPLAYER_SYNCH.md §B.2).\\n    return;\\n  }\\n\\n  if (state.isCollected) {\\n    // Silent reconciliation branch. Per MULTIPLAYER_SYNCH.md §6.2 rule 1, the canonical\\n    // feedback channel for remote collections is `collections[]` (see\\n    // `applyRemoteConfiguredCollections` below, which routes through the feedback variant).\\n    // If an `updates[]` row with `isCollected: true` arrives alongside or after that\\n    // event, firing feedback again would produce two particle bursts / two sounds. This\\n    // branch therefore stays silent.\\n    if (!CollectiblesManager.isCollectedInstance(parsed.localId)) {\\n      CollectiblesManager.applyRemoteCollected(parsed.localId);\\n    }\\n    return;\\n  }\\n\\n  const handle = CollectiblesManager.getMeshAndBody(parsed.localId);\\n  if (!handle) {\\n    // Item not yet loaded (async GLB still pending). The caller re-applies the\\n    // accumulated `knownItemStates` when `onEnvironmentItemsReady` fires, so this\\n    // is expected during the narrow env-load → items-ready window.\\n    return;\\n  }\\n\\n  ItemSync.applyRemoteItemState(handle.mesh, state, handle.body);\\n}\\n\\n/** Applies explicit `ItemCollectionEvent`s received from the server. */\\nexport function applyRemoteConfiguredCollections(\\n  currentEnvironmentName: string,\\n  collections: readonly ItemCollectionEvent[]\\n): void {\\n  for (const ev of collections) {\\n    const parsed = parseEnvScopedInstanceId(ev.instanceId);\\n    if (!parsed || parsed.envName !== currentEnvironmentName) {\\n      continue;\\n    }\\n    if (CollectiblesManager.isCollectedInstance(parsed.localId)) {\\n      continue;\\n    }\\n    // MULTIPLAYER_SYNCH.md §6.2 rule 1 \\\"Remote-collect feedback parity\\\": observers\\n    // play the same particle burst + spatialized collection sound as the collector.\\n    // No credits / inventory / scoring side-effects — those stay on the collector.\\n    CollectiblesManager.applyRemoteCollectedWithFeedback(parsed.localId);\\n  }\\n}\\n\",\"sync/environment_physics_sync.ts\":\"// ============================================================================\\n// ENVIRONMENT PHYSICS SYNC — replicate dynamic physicsObjects (Level Test cubes)\\n// ============================================================================\\n//\\n// Replicates transforms/velocities for `environment.physicsObjects` with mass > 0. Under the\\n// hybrid authority model these are **per-item authority** — each instance is owned by at most\\n// one client at a time and only its owner may publish rows. See MULTIPLAYER_SYNCH.md:\\n//   - §4.7 Item authority lifecycle\\n//     (../../../MULTIPLAYER_SYNCH.md#47-item-authority-lifecycle)\\n//   - §7.5 Item authority authorization\\n//     (../../../MULTIPLAYER_SYNCH.md#75-item-authority-authorization)\\n//\\n// `setEnvironmentPhysicsMeshKinematic()` flips individual bodies between ANIMATED\\n// (kinematic, receivers) and DYNAMIC (owner) as per-item authority changes.\\n\\nimport { ASSETS } from '../config/assets';\\nimport { applyPoseToMesh, sampleMeshPose } from '../utils/multiplayer_serialization';\\n\\nimport type { ItemInstanceState } from '../types/multiplayer';\\n\\n/** `ItemInstanceState.itemName` marker so clients route updates to meshes, not collectibles. */\\nexport const ENV_PHYSICS_ITEM_MARKER = '__env_physics__';\\n\\nexport function makeEnvironmentPhysicsInstanceId(\\n  environmentName: string,\\n  meshName: string\\n): string {\\n  return `${environmentName}::${meshName}`;\\n}\\n\\nexport function meshNameFromEnvironmentInstanceId(\\n  environmentName: string,\\n  instanceId: string\\n): string | null {\\n  const prefix = `${environmentName}::`;\\n  if (!instanceId.startsWith(prefix)) {\\n    return null;\\n  }\\n  const rest = instanceId.slice(prefix.length);\\n  return rest.trim() !== '' ? rest : null;\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Builds serializable snapshots for configured environment physics meshes (mass > 0).\\n * Each row carries the mesh pose as `{ pos, rot }` (Invariant P; no Euler, no separate\\n * velocity, no world matrix — see MULTIPLAYER_SYNCH.md §5.2).\\n */\\nexport function sampleEnvironmentPhysicsStates(\\n  scene: BABYLON.Scene,\\n  environmentName: string\\n): ItemInstanceState[] {\\n  const env = ASSETS.ENVIRONMENTS.find((e) => e.name === environmentName);\\n  if (!env || environmentName.trim() === '') {\\n    return [];\\n  }\\n\\n  const ts = Date.now();\\n  const out: ItemInstanceState[] = [];\\n\\n  for (const po of env.physicsObjects) {\\n    if (po.mass <= 0 || !po.name || po.name.trim() === '') {\\n      continue;\\n    }\\n    const mesh = scene.getMeshByName(po.name);\\n    if (!mesh || mesh.isDisposed()) {\\n      continue;\\n    }\\n\\n    const pose = sampleMeshPose(mesh);\\n    out.push({\\n      instanceId: makeEnvironmentPhysicsInstanceId(environmentName, po.name),\\n      itemName: ENV_PHYSICS_ITEM_MARKER,\\n      pos: pose.pos,\\n      rot: pose.rot,\\n      isCollected: false,\\n      timestamp: ts\\n    });\\n  }\\n\\n  return out;\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Applies an authoritative `{ pos, rot }` pose snapshot onto a replicated environment body.\\n *\\n * Per MULTIPLAYER_SYNCH.md §4.7 (non-owner kinematic invariant), Invariants P/E, and\\n * §B.9 (mesh-direct kinematic apply): the receiver writes the pose directly to the\\n * mesh's local channels (`mesh.position` + `mesh.rotationQuaternion`). On the next\\n * Havok tick the pre-step sync copies those channels onto the ANIMATED body, so\\n * collisions see the correct pose without any `setTargetTransform` interpolation or\\n * `disablePreStep` gymnastics, and without ever touching `mesh.scaling` (which is a\\n * static per-client spawn value).\\n *\\n * It MUST NOT call `setLinearVelocity` / `setAngularVelocity` (non-owner bodies are\\n * ANIMATED), MUST NOT call `setTargetTransform` (wrong primitive; see §B.9), and\\n * MUST skip DYNAMIC bodies (means the local client is the resolved owner; remote\\n * state for an owned item signals an authority mis-route upstream).\\n */\\nexport function applyRemoteEnvironmentPhysicsState(\\n  scene: BABYLON.Scene,\\n  currentEnvironmentName: string,\\n  state: ItemInstanceState\\n): void {\\n  try {\\n    if (!scene || scene.isDisposed) {\\n      return;\\n    }\\n  } catch {\\n    return;\\n  }\\n\\n  if (state.itemName !== ENV_PHYSICS_ITEM_MARKER) {\\n    return;\\n  }\\n\\n  if (\\n    !Array.isArray(state.pos) ||\\n    state.pos.length !== 3 ||\\n    !Array.isArray(state.rot) ||\\n    state.rot.length !== 4\\n  ) {\\n    return;\\n  }\\n\\n  const meshName = meshNameFromEnvironmentInstanceId(currentEnvironmentName, state.instanceId);\\n  if (!meshName) {\\n    return;\\n  }\\n\\n  const mesh = scene.getMeshByName(meshName);\\n  if (!mesh || mesh.isDisposed()) {\\n    return;\\n  }\\n\\n  const node = mesh as BABYLON.TransformNode & {\\n    physicsBody?: BABYLON.PhysicsBody;\\n  };\\n  const body = node.physicsBody;\\n\\n  if (body && !body.isDisposed && body.getMotionType() === BABYLON.PhysicsMotionType.DYNAMIC) {\\n    console.warn(\\n      `[EnvPhysicsSync] Received remote state for DYNAMIC (self-owned) env-physics item ${state.instanceId}; ignoring.`\\n    );\\n    return;\\n  }\\n\\n  try {\\n    applyPoseToMesh(mesh, { pos: state.pos, rot: state.rot });\\n  } catch {\\n    /* Havok/plugin edge — skip frame */\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Flips a single environment physics mesh's motion type. Used by the per-item authority\\n * pipeline so a dynamic mesh is kinematic on non-owners and dynamic on the owner.\\n * Returns true on a successful flip (or no-op when already in the requested state).\\n */\\nexport function setEnvironmentPhysicsMeshKinematic(\\n  scene: BABYLON.Scene,\\n  meshName: string,\\n  kinematic: boolean\\n): boolean {\\n  if (!meshName || meshName.trim() === '') {\\n    return false;\\n  }\\n  const mesh = scene.getMeshByName(meshName);\\n  if (!mesh || mesh.isDisposed()) {\\n    return false;\\n  }\\n  const node = mesh as BABYLON.TransformNode & { physicsBody?: BABYLON.PhysicsBody };\\n  const body = node.physicsBody;\\n  if (!body || body.isDisposed) {\\n    return false;\\n  }\\n  const motionType = kinematic\\n    ? BABYLON.PhysicsMotionType.ANIMATED\\n    : BABYLON.PhysicsMotionType.DYNAMIC;\\n  try {\\n    if (body.getMotionType() !== motionType) {\\n      body.setMotionType(motionType);\\n    }\\n    // Leave body.disablePreStep at its default (false). The non-owner apply path\\n    // writes mesh.position / rotationQuaternion directly (§B.9 mesh-direct\\n    // kinematic pattern); we need the pre-step sync enabled so those writes\\n    // reach the body before each physics step.\\n    if (kinematic) {\\n      body.setLinearVelocity(BABYLON.Vector3.Zero());\\n      body.setAngularVelocity(BABYLON.Vector3.Zero());\\n    }\\n    return true;\\n  } catch {\\n    return false;\\n  }\\n}\\n\",\"sync/item_authority_tracker.ts\":\"// ============================================================================\\n// ITEM AUTHORITY TRACKER — mirror of server's itemOwners map for this client\\n// ============================================================================\\n//\\n// Consumes `item-authority-changed` SSE signals (MULTIPLAYER_SYNCH.md §6.8) to maintain a\\n// `Map<instanceId, ownerClientId | null>`. Emits local change callbacks so the bootstrap can\\n// flip physics motion types per-item (dynamic for self-owned, kinematic for peer-owned).\\n//\\n// This is a pure, deterministic state mirror — it never talks to the network directly; the\\n// caller wires it up to `MultiplayerManager.on('item-authority-changed', …)` and optimistic\\n// post-claim updates.\\n\\nimport type { ItemAuthorityChangedMessage } from '../types/multiplayer';\\n\\nexport type AuthorityChangeListener = (evt: {\\n  instanceId: string;\\n  previousOwnerId: string | null;\\n  newOwnerId: string | null;\\n  selfOwnsNow: boolean;\\n  selfOwnedBefore: boolean;\\n  reason: string;\\n}) => void;\\n\\nexport class ItemAuthorityTracker {\\n  private owners = new Map<string, string>();\\n  private selfClientId: string | null = null;\\n  private listeners = new Set<AuthorityChangeListener>();\\n  /**\\n   * Set of env names for which an authority snapshot has been applied (either via the\\n   * SSE-open replay from `pushAuthoritySnapshotToSession` landing on the wire, or via\\n   * the bootstrap `item-state-update` that follows env-entry). Used by\\n   * {@link multiplayer_bootstrap} to gate publish-side fallbacks — see\\n   * MULTIPLAYER_SYNCH.md §4.8 *No-authority-means-non-owner*: a client MUST NOT assume\\n   * ambient self-ownership (including the \\\"unowned + base-synchronizer\\\" publish fallback)\\n   * until it has applied at least one authority signal for the env in question.\\n   */\\n  private snapshotAppliedEnvs = new Set<string>();\\n  /**\\n   * Per-environment authority mirror (§4.8). envAuthority[envName] = clientId of the\\n   * current env-authority as reported by the server via `env-item-authority-changed`.\\n   * Used by {@link isOwnedBySelf} to resolve unowned items: when self is the env-authority\\n   * for an item's env and the item has no explicit `itemOwners` entry, self is the resolved\\n   * owner and the item should run DYNAMIC.\\n   */\\n  private envAuthority = new Map<string, string>();\\n\\n  public setSelfClientId(id: string | null): void {\\n    this.selfClientId = id;\\n  }\\n\\n  public getSelfClientId(): string | null {\\n    return this.selfClientId;\\n  }\\n\\n  /** Register a listener; returns an unsubscribe function. */\\n  public onChange(listener: AuthorityChangeListener): () => void {\\n    this.listeners.add(listener);\\n    return () => {\\n      this.listeners.delete(listener);\\n    };\\n  }\\n\\n  public getOwner(instanceId: string): string | null {\\n    return this.owners.get(instanceId) ?? null;\\n  }\\n\\n  /**\\n   * Resolves whether `instanceId` is currently under the local client's authority, using\\n   * the full three-tier resolution described in MULTIPLAYER_SYNCH.md §4 (explicit owner →\\n   * env-authority → nobody):\\n   *\\n   * 1. If `itemOwners[instanceId]` names self → `true` (§4.7 explicit claim).\\n   * 2. If no explicit owner exists AND `envAuthority[envName]` names self → `true`\\n   *    (§4.8 env-authority: self drives all unclaimed items in this env).\\n   * 3. Otherwise → `false`.\\n   *\\n   * **Invariant (MULTIPLAYER_SYNCH.md §4.8 *No-authority-means-non-owner*).** In the\\n   * absence of any applied authority signal for the item's environment, both the explicit\\n   * owner lookup (tier 1) and the env-authority lookup (tier 2) return falsy, so the\\n   * result is `false`. This correctly seeds newcomers into ANIMATED-default and prevents\\n   * the \\\"both DYNAMIC, no propagation\\\" failure mode.\\n   *\\n   * The env name is extracted from the env-scoped instanceId prefix (format \\\"envName::…\\\").\\n   */\\n  public isOwnedBySelf(instanceId: string): boolean {\\n    const self = this.selfClientId;\\n    if (!self) {\\n      return false;\\n    }\\n    const explicitOwner = this.owners.get(instanceId);\\n    if (explicitOwner !== undefined) {\\n      return explicitOwner === self;\\n    }\\n    // No explicit owner — fall back to env-authority tier.\\n    const envName = instanceId.includes('::') ? instanceId.split('::')[0] : '';\\n    if (!envName) {\\n      return false;\\n    }\\n    const envAuth = this.envAuthority.get(envName);\\n    return envAuth === self;\\n  }\\n\\n  /**\\n   * Apply an `env-item-authority-changed` signal from the server. Records the new\\n   * env-authority for `envName` so that {@link isOwnedBySelf} can resolve unowned items\\n   * in that environment using the §4.8 env-authority tier.\\n   * Returns `true` if the env-authority transitioned (i.e. the value changed), allowing\\n   * callers to decide whether to re-seed motion types.\\n   */\\n  public applyEnvAuthorityChange(envName: string, newAuthorityId: string | null): boolean {\\n    if (!envName) {\\n      return false;\\n    }\\n    const prev = this.envAuthority.get(envName) ?? null;\\n    if (newAuthorityId) {\\n      this.envAuthority.set(envName, newAuthorityId);\\n    } else {\\n      this.envAuthority.delete(envName);\\n    }\\n    return prev !== (newAuthorityId ?? null);\\n  }\\n\\n  /** Returns the current env-authority client ID for `envName`, or null if none. */\\n  public getEnvAuthority(envName: string): string | null {\\n    return this.envAuthority.get(envName) ?? null;\\n  }\\n\\n  public isUnowned(instanceId: string): boolean {\\n    return !this.owners.has(instanceId);\\n  }\\n\\n  /**\\n   * Records that an authority snapshot has been applied for `envName`. Called by the\\n   * bootstrap after `mp.join()` resolves and the server's `pushAuthoritySnapshotToSession`\\n   * replay has had a chance to land (see MULTIPLAYER_SYNCH.md §4.5 SSE-open ordering).\\n   * Also called on receipt of the first `item-state-update` for `envName` after env-entry\\n   * (§5.2.2 rule 4 AOI-enter bootstrap).\\n   */\\n  public markSnapshotApplied(envName: string): void {\\n    if (!envName) {\\n      return;\\n    }\\n    this.snapshotAppliedEnvs.add(envName);\\n  }\\n\\n  /**\\n   * Reports whether {@link markSnapshotApplied} has been called for `envName`. Used by\\n   * the publish gate to refuse the \\\"unowned + base-synchronizer\\\" fallback until the\\n   * authority signal for the env has been observed — otherwise a racing newcomer could\\n   * publish rows for items the server has already assigned to another client (producing\\n   * a false claim on the server side).\\n   */\\n  public hasSnapshotAppliedFor(envName: string): boolean {\\n    return this.snapshotAppliedEnvs.has(envName);\\n  }\\n\\n  /** Clears snapshot-applied state (e.g. on env-switch — next env must re-absorb). */\\n  public clearSnapshotAppliedFor(envName: string): void {\\n    this.snapshotAppliedEnvs.delete(envName);\\n  }\\n\\n  public getAllOwnedBySelf(): string[] {\\n    if (!this.selfClientId) {\\n      return [];\\n    }\\n    const out: string[] = [];\\n    for (const [id, owner] of this.owners) {\\n      if (owner === this.selfClientId) {\\n        out.push(id);\\n      }\\n    }\\n    return out;\\n  }\\n\\n  /** Apply the authoritative server transition. Fires onChange only when state actually flips. */\\n  public applyAuthorityChange(msg: ItemAuthorityChangedMessage): void {\\n    const { instanceId } = msg;\\n    if (!instanceId) {\\n      return;\\n    }\\n    const previous = this.owners.get(instanceId) ?? null;\\n    if (msg.newOwnerId) {\\n      this.owners.set(instanceId, msg.newOwnerId);\\n    } else {\\n      this.owners.delete(instanceId);\\n    }\\n    if (previous === (msg.newOwnerId ?? null)) {\\n      return;\\n    }\\n    const self = this.selfClientId;\\n    const selfOwnsNow = !!self && msg.newOwnerId === self;\\n    const selfOwnedBefore = !!self && previous === self;\\n    this.fire({\\n      instanceId,\\n      previousOwnerId: previous,\\n      newOwnerId: msg.newOwnerId ?? null,\\n      selfOwnsNow,\\n      selfOwnedBefore,\\n      reason: msg.reason\\n    });\\n  }\\n\\n  /**\\n   * Optimistic local set used right after firing a claim: we assume the server will accept\\n   * if the item was unowned. If the accept response shows otherwise, call\\n   * {@link applyAuthorityChange} with the server's view to correct.\\n   */\\n  public optimisticSetSelfOwner(instanceId: string): void {\\n    if (!this.selfClientId) {\\n      return;\\n    }\\n    const prev = this.owners.get(instanceId) ?? null;\\n    if (prev === this.selfClientId) {\\n      return;\\n    }\\n    this.owners.set(instanceId, this.selfClientId);\\n    this.fire({\\n      instanceId,\\n      previousOwnerId: prev,\\n      newOwnerId: this.selfClientId,\\n      selfOwnsNow: true,\\n      selfOwnedBefore: false,\\n      reason: 'claim'\\n    });\\n  }\\n\\n  /** Clear tracker (e.g. on disconnect / env change if desired). */\\n  public clear(): void {\\n    if (this.owners.size === 0) {\\n      return;\\n    }\\n    const ids = Array.from(this.owners.keys());\\n    const self = this.selfClientId;\\n    this.owners.clear();\\n    for (const id of ids) {\\n      this.fire({\\n        instanceId: id,\\n        previousOwnerId: null,\\n        newOwnerId: null,\\n        selfOwnsNow: false,\\n        selfOwnedBefore: self !== null,\\n        reason: 'release'\\n      });\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  private fire(evt: Parameters<AuthorityChangeListener>[0]): void {\\n    for (const l of this.listeners) {\\n      try {\\n        l(evt);\\n      } catch (e) {\\n        console.warn('[ItemAuthorityTracker] listener threw:', e);\\n      }\\n    }\\n  }\\n}\\n\",\"sync/item_sync.ts\":\"// ============================================================================\\n// ITEM STATE SYNC MODULE\\n// ============================================================================\\n//\\n// Item replication under the hybrid authority model. See MULTIPLAYER_SYNCH.md:\\n//   - §4.7 Item authority lifecycle\\n//     (../../../MULTIPLAYER_SYNCH.md#47-item-authority-lifecycle)\\n//   - §5.2 Item state (per-row filter)\\n//     (../../../MULTIPLAYER_SYNCH.md#52-item-state)\\n//   - §7.5 Item authority authorization\\n//     (../../../MULTIPLAYER_SYNCH.md#75-item-authority-authorization)\\n//\\n// This module is the low-level abstraction that tracks per-instance snapshots and applies\\n// remote snapshots onto local meshes. Per MULTIPLAYER_SYNCH.md §B.9 (mesh-direct kinematic\\n// apply), the apply path writes `mesh.position` + `mesh.rotationQuaternion` directly;\\n// Havok's pre-step sync then propagates the pose onto the ANIMATED physics body before\\n// the next physics tick. It is intentionally authority-agnostic: callers (e.g.\\n// `configured_items_sync.ts`) decide whether the local client currently owns an instanceId\\n// and may publish rows for it.\\n\\nimport { applyPoseToMesh, ThrottledFunction } from '../utils/multiplayer_serialization';\\n\\nimport type { ItemInstanceState, ItemStateUpdate, ItemCollectionEvent } from '../types/multiplayer';\\n\\n/**\\n * Tracks and detects item state changes for multiplayer replication.\\n *\\n * Authority-agnostic: this class does not gate sends on the base-synchronizer role. The caller\\n * is responsible for only publishing rows for `instanceId`s the local client currently owns,\\n * per [MULTIPLAYER_SYNCH.md §4.7](../../../MULTIPLAYER_SYNCH.md#47-item-authority-lifecycle).\\n * Rows published without authority are silently dropped server-side (§7.5) rather than\\n * rejected.\\n */\\nexport class ItemSync {\\n  private itemStates = new Map<string, ItemInstanceState>();\\n  private collectionEvents: ItemCollectionEvent[] = [];\\n  private throttle: ThrottledFunction;\\n\\n  constructor(throttleMs = 100) {\\n    this.throttle = new ThrottledFunction(throttleMs);\\n  }\\n\\n  /**\\n   * Updates item state snapshot\\n   */\\n  public updateItemState(state: ItemInstanceState): void {\\n    this.itemStates.set(state.instanceId, state);\\n  }\\n\\n  /**\\n   * Records item collection event\\n   */\\n  public recordCollection(event: ItemCollectionEvent): void {\\n    this.collectionEvents.push(event);\\n  }\\n\\n  /**\\n   * Creates state update and clears pending events\\n   */\\n  public createStateUpdate(timestamp: number): ItemStateUpdate | null {\\n    if (!this.throttle.shouldCall()) {\\n      return null;\\n    }\\n\\n    if (this.itemStates.size === 0 && this.collectionEvents.length === 0) {\\n      return null;\\n    }\\n\\n    const update: ItemStateUpdate = {\\n      updates: Array.from(this.itemStates.values()),\\n      collections: this.collectionEvents.length > 0 ? [...this.collectionEvents] : undefined,\\n      timestamp\\n    };\\n\\n    // Clear pending events after creating update\\n    this.collectionEvents = [];\\n\\n    return update;\\n  }\\n\\n  /**\\n   * Gets all current item states\\n   */\\n  public getAllItemStates(): ItemInstanceState[] {\\n    return Array.from(this.itemStates.values());\\n  }\\n\\n  /**\\n   * Applies a remote item snapshot to a mesh (and its kinematic physics body if present).\\n   *\\n   * Wire contract (Invariants P and E, MULTIPLAYER_SYNCH.md §5.2): `state.pos` is the\\n   * world-space position and `state.rot` is the pre-scale unit quaternion, both read\\n   * from the owner's local mesh channels and written verbatim onto the replica's local\\n   * channels. No matrix decomposition on either side; `mesh.scaling` is never touched\\n   * because it is a static per-client config value (see §5.2 pose-only transport).\\n   *\\n   * Apply strategy (MULTIPLAYER_SYNCH.md §B.9 — kinematic apply pattern):\\n   *   - **Never** call `setTargetTransform` on the physics body. `setTargetTransform`\\n   *     is designed for authored kinematic animation (moving platforms etc.); when\\n   *     fed with a new replica target every tick it produces the \\\"whizzing demon\\\"\\n   *     artefact on P2 as the interpolator is retargeted before it reaches each\\n   *     prior target.\\n   *   - **Never** toggle `body.disablePreStep`. The default (`false`) lets Havok's\\n   *     pre-step copy `mesh.position` / `mesh.rotationQuaternion` onto the body\\n   *     before each step — i.e. the canonical ANIMATED-body drive path.\\n   *   - **Never** call `setLinearVelocity` / `setAngularVelocity` — non-owner\\n   *     bodies are ANIMATED (kinematic) per the non-owner kinematic invariant\\n   *     (§4.7) and integrating velocities would violate that.\\n   *   - Euler channels (`mesh.rotation`) are never written (Invariant E).\\n   *\\n   * Short-circuit on DYNAMIC bodies: a DYNAMIC motion type implies the local client\\n   * IS the resolved owner. Receiving a remote snapshot for an owned item signals an\\n   * authority-routing bug upstream; blindly writing its pose would fight the local\\n   * simulation. We ignore and log so the mismatch is visible.\\n   */\\n  public static applyRemoteItemState(\\n    itemMesh: BABYLON.AbstractMesh,\\n    state: ItemInstanceState,\\n    body?: BABYLON.PhysicsAggregate | null\\n  ): void {\\n    if (!itemMesh) return;\\n\\n    const physicsBody =\\n      body && !body.body.isDisposed ? body.body : this.resolveMeshPhysicsBody(itemMesh);\\n\\n    if (!state.isCollected) {\\n      const hasPose =\\n        Array.isArray(state.pos) &&\\n        state.pos.length === 3 &&\\n        Array.isArray(state.rot) &&\\n        state.rot.length === 4;\\n      if (!hasPose) {\\n        // Collection status still needs to be applied below.\\n      } else if (\\n        physicsBody &&\\n        !physicsBody.isDisposed &&\\n        physicsBody.getMotionType() === BABYLON.PhysicsMotionType.DYNAMIC\\n      ) {\\n        // We own this body (DYNAMIC = local simulation). Remote state must not\\n        // be written; it would compete with our own physics step. The server's\\n        // owner-pin rule is supposed to prevent this from arriving, so log.\\n        console.warn(\\n          `[ItemSync] Received remote state for DYNAMIC (self-owned) item ${state.instanceId}; ignoring.`\\n        );\\n      } else {\\n        // ANIMATED (kinematic) or no body: write the pose (pos + rotationQuaternion)\\n        // directly onto the mesh. On the next physics tick, Havok's pre-step sync\\n        // (disablePreStep=false) copies those channels onto the body so collision\\n        // queries see the correct pose without any interpolation surprises.\\n        try {\\n          applyPoseToMesh(itemMesh, { pos: state.pos, rot: state.rot });\\n        } catch (e) {\\n          console.warn('[ItemSync] Failed to apply pose:', e);\\n        }\\n      }\\n    }\\n\\n    try {\\n      if (state.isCollected) {\\n        itemMesh.isVisible = false;\\n        itemMesh.setEnabled(false);\\n      } else {\\n        itemMesh.isVisible = true;\\n        itemMesh.setEnabled(true);\\n      }\\n    } catch (e) {\\n      console.warn('[ItemSync] Failed to apply collection status:', e);\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  private static resolveMeshPhysicsBody(mesh: BABYLON.AbstractMesh): BABYLON.PhysicsBody | null {\\n    const maybe = (mesh as BABYLON.AbstractMesh & { physicsBody?: BABYLON.PhysicsBody })\\n      .physicsBody;\\n    if (maybe && !maybe.isDisposed) {\\n      return maybe;\\n    }\\n    return null;\\n  }\\n\\n  /**\\n   * Marks item collected (typically called by managers handling collection logic)\\n   * @param instanceId The item instance ID\\n   * @param collected Whether the item is collected\\n   */\\n  public markItemCollected(instanceId: string, collected: boolean): void {\\n    const state = this.itemStates.get(instanceId);\\n    if (state) {\\n      // Create updated state with collection status\\n      const updated: ItemInstanceState = {\\n        ...state,\\n        isCollected: collected,\\n        collectedByClientId: collected ? state.collectedByClientId : undefined\\n      };\\n      this.itemStates.set(instanceId, updated);\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  /**\\n   * Removes item state (when item is permanently removed from world)\\n   */\\n  public removeItemState(instanceId: string): void {\\n    this.itemStates.delete(instanceId);\\n  }\\n\\n  /**\\n   * Clears all tracked items (for scene switch)\\n   */\\n  public clearAll(): void {\\n    this.itemStates.clear();\\n    this.collectionEvents = [];\\n  }\\n}\\n\",\"sync/multiplayer_wire_guards.ts\":\"// ============================================================================\\n// MULTIPLAYER WIRE GUARDS — validate/coerce SSE & REST payloads defensively\\n// ============================================================================\\n\\nimport { normalizeQuaternion } from '../utils/multiplayer_serialization';\\n\\nimport type {\\n  CharacterState,\\n  ItemInstanceState,\\n  QuaternionSerializable\\n} from '../types/multiplayer';\\n\\n/** World-space limits (meters); rejects absurd/grief payloads before Havok sees them. */\\nconst MAX_ABS_WORLD_COORD = 5e6;\\n\\nexport function clampCoordComponent(n: number): number {\\n  if (!Number.isFinite(n)) {\\n    return 0;\\n  }\\n  return Math.max(-MAX_ABS_WORLD_COORD, Math.min(MAX_ABS_WORLD_COORD, n));\\n}\\n\\n/** Position or linear velocity in world units (clamped). */\\nfunction coerceWorldVector3(raw: unknown): [number, number, number] | null {\\n  if (!Array.isArray(raw) || raw.length !== 3) {\\n    return null;\\n  }\\n  const a = Number(raw[0]);\\n  const b = Number(raw[1]);\\n  const c = Number(raw[2]);\\n  if (!Number.isFinite(a) || !Number.isFinite(b) || !Number.isFinite(c)) {\\n    return null;\\n  }\\n  return [clampCoordComponent(a), clampCoordComponent(b), clampCoordComponent(c)];\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Wire rotation for **character sync** and **item sync**: unit quaternion [x, y, z, w].\\n * Items carry a `pos` + `rot` pose pair on the wire — Invariant P in\\n * [MULTIPLAYER_SYNCH.md §5.2](../../../MULTIPLAYER_SYNCH.md#52-item-state). `rot` is\\n * validated and normalized via this function.\\n */\\nfunction coerceQuaternion(raw: unknown): QuaternionSerializable | null {\\n  if (!Array.isArray(raw) || raw.length !== 4) {\\n    return null;\\n  }\\n  const x = Number(raw[0]);\\n  const y = Number(raw[1]);\\n  const z = Number(raw[2]);\\n  const w = Number(raw[3]);\\n  if (!Number.isFinite(x) || !Number.isFinite(y) || !Number.isFinite(z) || !Number.isFinite(w)) {\\n    return null;\\n  }\\n  return normalizeQuaternion([x, y, z, w]);\\n}\\n\\nexport function coerceItemInstanceState(raw: unknown): ItemInstanceState | null {\\n  if (raw === null || typeof raw !== 'object') {\\n    return null;\\n  }\\n  const rawInstanceId = Reflect.get(raw, 'instanceId');\\n  const rawItemName = Reflect.get(raw, 'itemName');\\n  const instanceId = typeof rawInstanceId === 'string' ? rawInstanceId.trim() : '';\\n  const itemName = typeof rawItemName === 'string' ? rawItemName.trim() : '';\\n  if (!instanceId || !itemName) {\\n    return null;\\n  }\\n\\n  const rawIsCollected = Reflect.get(raw, 'isCollected');\\n  const isCollected = Boolean(rawIsCollected);\\n\\n  // Invariant P (pose-only): a live (non-collected) row MUST carry a valid `pos` and\\n  // `rot`. Collection-only rows (isCollected=true) are tolerated without a pose\\n  // because the receiver hides the mesh and the transform is irrelevant.\\n  const pos = coerceWorldVector3(Reflect.get(raw, 'pos'));\\n  const rot = coerceQuaternion(Reflect.get(raw, 'rot'));\\n  if ((!pos || !rot) && !isCollected) {\\n    return null;\\n  }\\n\\n  const tsNum = Number(Reflect.get(raw, 'timestamp'));\\n  const timestamp = Number.isFinite(tsNum) ? tsNum : Date.now();\\n\\n  let collectedBy: string | undefined;\\n  const rawCollectedBy = Reflect.get(raw, 'collectedByClientId');\\n  if (typeof rawCollectedBy === 'string' && rawCollectedBy.trim() !== '') {\\n    collectedBy = rawCollectedBy.trim();\\n  }\\n\\n  let ownerClientId: string | null | undefined;\\n  const rawOwnerClientId = Reflect.get(raw, 'ownerClientId');\\n  if (typeof rawOwnerClientId === 'string' && rawOwnerClientId.trim() !== '') {\\n    ownerClientId = rawOwnerClientId.trim();\\n  } else if (rawOwnerClientId === null) {\\n    ownerClientId = null;\\n  }\\n\\n  return {\\n    instanceId,\\n    itemName,\\n    pos: pos ?? [0, 0, 0],\\n    rot: rot ?? [0, 0, 0, 1],\\n    isCollected,\\n    collectedByClientId: collectedBy,\\n    ownerClientId,\\n    timestamp\\n  };\\n}\\n\\nexport function coerceCharacterState(raw: unknown): CharacterState | null {\\n  if (raw === null || typeof raw !== 'object') {\\n    return null;\\n  }\\n  const rawClientId = Reflect.get(raw, 'clientId');\\n  const clientId = typeof rawClientId === 'string' ? rawClientId.trim() : '';\\n  if (!clientId) {\\n    return null;\\n  }\\n\\n  const rawCharacterModelId = Reflect.get(raw, 'characterModelId');\\n  let characterModelId = typeof rawCharacterModelId === 'string' ? rawCharacterModelId.trim() : '';\\n  if (!characterModelId) {\\n    characterModelId = 'Red';\\n  }\\n\\n  const rawEnvironmentName = Reflect.get(raw, 'environmentName');\\n  const environmentName = typeof rawEnvironmentName === 'string' ? rawEnvironmentName.trim() : '';\\n\\n  const position = coerceWorldVector3(Reflect.get(raw, 'position'));\\n  const rotation = coerceQuaternion(Reflect.get(raw, 'rotation'));\\n  const velocity = coerceWorldVector3(Reflect.get(raw, 'velocity'));\\n  if (!position || !rotation || !velocity) {\\n    return null;\\n  }\\n\\n  const rawAnimationState = Reflect.get(raw, 'animationState');\\n  let animationState = typeof rawAnimationState === 'string' ? rawAnimationState.trim() : '';\\n  if (!animationState) {\\n    animationState = 'idle';\\n  }\\n\\n  const afNum = Number(Reflect.get(raw, 'animationFrame'));\\n  const animationFrame = Number.isFinite(afNum) ? Math.min(1, Math.max(0, afNum)) : 0;\\n\\n  const isJumping = Boolean(Reflect.get(raw, 'isJumping'));\\n  const isBoosting = Boolean(Reflect.get(raw, 'isBoosting'));\\n\\n  let boostType: 'superJump' | 'invisibility' | undefined;\\n  const rawBoostType = Reflect.get(raw, 'boostType');\\n  if (rawBoostType === 'superJump' || rawBoostType === 'invisibility') {\\n    boostType = rawBoostType;\\n  }\\n\\n  const btNum = Number(Reflect.get(raw, 'boostTimeRemaining'));\\n  const boostTimeRemaining = Number.isFinite(btNum) ? Math.max(0, btNum) : 0;\\n\\n  const tsNum = Number(Reflect.get(raw, 'timestamp'));\\n  const timestamp = Number.isFinite(tsNum) ? tsNum : Date.now();\\n\\n  return {\\n    clientId,\\n    environmentName,\\n    characterModelId,\\n    position,\\n    rotation,\\n    velocity,\\n    animationState,\\n    animationFrame,\\n    isJumping,\\n    isBoosting,\\n    boostType,\\n    boostTimeRemaining,\\n    timestamp\\n  };\\n}\\n\",\"sync/proximity_claim_observer.ts\":\"// ============================================================================\\n// PROXIMITY CLAIM OBSERVER — fires item authority claims before collision\\n// ============================================================================\\n//\\n// Drives the per-item authority model (MULTIPLAYER_SYNCH.md §4.7) on the client side:\\n//   - When the local character enters a `claimRadiusMeters` bubble around a dynamic\\n//     item, the observer PATCHes `/item-authority-claim` so the body is already owned\\n//     (and can be flipped to DYNAMIC) by the time the capsule touches the item.\\n//   - When the character leaves the bubble, a grace timer starts; if the character does\\n//     not re-enter within `claimGraceMs`, the observer releases ownership.\\n//   - Owners keep publishing rows via the existing item-state pipeline; the server's idle\\n//     timeout picks up stragglers (e.g. tab freeze) per §4.7 rule 2c.\\n//\\n// The observer is authority-UI: it does not flip physics motion types itself — the\\n// bootstrap listens to `ItemAuthorityTracker` change events to do that, so optimistic\\n// claim + server confirmation follow a single code path.\\n\\nimport type { ItemAuthorityTracker } from './item_authority_tracker';\\nimport type { MultiplayerManager } from '../managers/multiplayer_manager';\\n\\nexport interface ProximityItem {\\n  /** Wire instance id (env-scoped). */\\n  readonly instanceId: string;\\n  /** Per-frame position source; returns null if the item is gone / collected. */\\n  readonly getPosition: () => BABYLON.Vector3 | null;\\n}\\n\\nexport interface ProximityClaimObserverOptions {\\n  readonly claimRadiusMeters: number;\\n  readonly claimGraceMs: number;\\n  readonly getCharacterPosition: () => BABYLON.Vector3 | null;\\n  /** Optional: skip claim attempts while true (e.g. scene switching). */\\n  readonly shouldPause?: () => boolean;\\n}\\n\\ninterface ProxState {\\n  inBubble: boolean;\\n  exitedAt: number | null;\\n  lastClaimAttemptAt: number;\\n  claimInFlight: boolean;\\n}\\n\\nexport class ProximityClaimObserver {\\n  private items = new Map<string, ProximityItem>();\\n  private state = new Map<string, ProxState>();\\n\\n  constructor(\\n    private readonly mp: MultiplayerManager,\\n    private readonly tracker: ItemAuthorityTracker,\\n    private readonly opts: ProximityClaimObserverOptions\\n  ) {}\\n\\n  /** Replace the set of items the observer tracks; usually called on env load. */\\n  public setItems(items: readonly ProximityItem[]): void {\\n    const incoming = new Set(items.map((i) => i.instanceId));\\n    for (const id of Array.from(this.items.keys())) {\\n      if (!incoming.has(id)) {\\n        this.items.delete(id);\\n        this.state.delete(id);\\n      }\\n    }\\n    for (const it of items) {\\n      this.items.set(it.instanceId, it);\\n      if (!this.state.has(it.instanceId)) {\\n        this.state.set(it.instanceId, {\\n          inBubble: false,\\n          exitedAt: null,\\n          lastClaimAttemptAt: 0,\\n          claimInFlight: false\\n        });\\n      }\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  public clear(): void {\\n    this.items.clear();\\n    this.state.clear();\\n  }\\n\\n  /**\\n   * Per-frame update. Call from `scene.onBeforeRenderObservable`.\\n   * Returns the number of items currently inside the bubble (mostly for diagnostics).\\n   */\\n  public tick(): number {\\n    if (this.opts.shouldPause?.()) {\\n      return 0;\\n    }\\n    const charPos = this.opts.getCharacterPosition();\\n    if (!charPos) {\\n      return 0;\\n    }\\n    const self = this.tracker.getSelfClientId();\\n    if (!self) {\\n      return 0;\\n    }\\n\\n    const now = performance.now();\\n    const rSq = this.opts.claimRadiusMeters * this.opts.claimRadiusMeters;\\n    const graceMs = this.opts.claimGraceMs;\\n    let inside = 0;\\n\\n    for (const [id, item] of this.items) {\\n      const p = item.getPosition();\\n      const st = this.state.get(id);\\n      if (!p || !st) {\\n        continue;\\n      }\\n      const dx = p.x - charPos.x;\\n      const dy = p.y - charPos.y;\\n      const dz = p.z - charPos.z;\\n      const distSq = dx * dx + dy * dy + dz * dz;\\n      const isInside = distSq <= rSq;\\n      if (isInside) {\\n        inside++;\\n      }\\n\\n      if (isInside && !st.inBubble) {\\n        st.inBubble = true;\\n        st.exitedAt = null;\\n        this.maybeClaim(id, charPos, now, st, self);\\n      } else if (isInside && st.inBubble) {\\n        if (\\n          !this.tracker.isOwnedBySelf(id) &&\\n          !st.claimInFlight &&\\n          now - st.lastClaimAttemptAt > 400\\n        ) {\\n          this.maybeClaim(id, charPos, now, st, self);\\n        }\\n      } else if (!isInside && st.inBubble) {\\n        st.inBubble = false;\\n        st.exitedAt = now;\\n      } else if (!isInside && st.exitedAt !== null) {\\n        if (this.tracker.isOwnedBySelf(id) && now - st.exitedAt >= graceMs) {\\n          st.exitedAt = null;\\n          void this.mp.releaseItemAuthority(id, { reason: 'grace_expired' });\\n        } else if (!this.tracker.isOwnedBySelf(id)) {\\n          st.exitedAt = null;\\n        }\\n      }\\n    }\\n\\n    return inside;\\n  }\\n\\n  private maybeClaim(\\n    id: string,\\n    charPos: BABYLON.Vector3,\\n    now: number,\\n    st: ProxState,\\n    selfClientId: string\\n  ): void {\\n    if (st.claimInFlight) {\\n      return;\\n    }\\n    if (this.tracker.isOwnedBySelf(id)) {\\n      return;\\n    }\\n    st.claimInFlight = true;\\n    st.lastClaimAttemptAt = now;\\n\\n    // Track whether THIS call was the one that flipped local state to self.\\n    // `isUnowned` must be sampled BEFORE the optimistic flip because\\n    // `optimisticSetSelfOwner` writes `owners.set(instanceId, selfClientId)`,\\n    // which would make `isUnowned` lie on a subsequent read.\\n    const didOptimistic = this.tracker.isUnowned(id);\\n    if (didOptimistic) {\\n      this.tracker.optimisticSetSelfOwner(id);\\n    }\\n\\n    const payload = {\\n      clientPosition: { x: charPos.x, y: charPos.y, z: charPos.z },\\n      reason: 'proximity'\\n    };\\n\\n    void this.mp\\n      .claimItemAuthority(id, payload)\\n      .then((resp) => {\\n        // Case A: network/non-OK (resp === null). If we flipped optimistic,\\n        // revert — otherwise the client stays pinned as owner forever, flips\\n        // its body to DYNAMIC, and its ItemSync keeps replaying a row the\\n        // server will mark \\\"unauthorized\\\" every throttle window.\\n        if (!resp) {\\n          if (didOptimistic) {\\n            // Use reason='release' (the only non-claim, non-system reason in\\n            // ItemAuthorityChangedMessage) to represent a locally-initiated\\n            // revert of a never-confirmed optimistic claim. No signal is sent\\n            // over the network; this is a tracker-local correction only.\\n            this.tracker.applyAuthorityChange({\\n              instanceId: id,\\n              previousOwnerId: selfClientId,\\n              newOwnerId: null,\\n              reason: 'release',\\n              timestamp: Date.now()\\n            });\\n          }\\n          return;\\n        }\\n\\n        // Case B: server accepted our claim (possibly after idle-timeout\\n        // takeover). The authoritative `item-authority-changed` SSE signal\\n        // will still land; nothing to revert here.\\n        if (resp.accepted) {\\n          return;\\n        }\\n\\n        // Case C: server rejected AND named a different current owner.\\n        // Apply the server's view directly; this also replaces our optimistic\\n        // self-owner entry in `owners` with the real owner.\\n        if (resp.currentOwnerId && resp.currentOwnerId !== selfClientId) {\\n          this.tracker.applyAuthorityChange({\\n            instanceId: id,\\n            previousOwnerId: selfClientId,\\n            newOwnerId: resp.currentOwnerId,\\n            reason: 'claim',\\n            timestamp: resp.serverTimestamp\\n          });\\n          return;\\n        }\\n\\n        // Case D: ambiguous reject (no currentOwnerId, or it echoes self).\\n        // Safest recovery is to drop the optimistic flip so authority is\\n        // re-derived from env-authority / explicit ownership and the item's\\n        // body is returned to ANIMATED until a real claim succeeds.\\n        if (didOptimistic) {\\n          this.tracker.applyAuthorityChange({\\n            instanceId: id,\\n            previousOwnerId: selfClientId,\\n            newOwnerId: null,\\n            reason: 'release',\\n            timestamp: resp.serverTimestamp\\n          });\\n        }\\n      })\\n      .finally(() => {\\n        st.claimInFlight = false;\\n      });\\n  }\\n}\\n\",\"types/audio.ts\":\"/**\\n * Volume ramp options aligned with Babylon.js Audio V2\\n * {@link BABYLON.AbstractSound.setVolume} second parameter.\\n *\\n * Declared here instead of importing from `@babylonjs/core/AudioV2/audioParameter`\\n * so exports work in the official Playground (its resolver often mismatches deep\\n * subpath typings for `IAudioParameterRampOptions`).\\n *\\n * Babylon's `Nullable<T>` is not imported from `@babylonjs/core/types` here because\\n * the official Playground does not expose that as a named export on the `types`\\n * entry; use `| null` in public signatures instead.\\n */\\nexport interface AudioVolumeRampOptions {\\n  duration: number;\\n  shape: 'linear' | 'exponential' | 'logarithmic' | 'none';\\n}\\n\\nexport interface ManagedAudioSound {\\n  play(): void;\\n  stop(): void;\\n  dispose(): void;\\n  setVolume(volume: number, ramp?: Partial<AudioVolumeRampOptions> | null): void;\\n  getVolume(): number;\\n  isActive(): boolean;\\n}\\n\\ntype AbstractSoundVolumeRamp = Parameters<BABYLON.AbstractSound['setVolume']>[1];\\n\\nfunction toEngineVolumeRamp(\\n  ramp: Partial<AudioVolumeRampOptions> | null | undefined\\n): AbstractSoundVolumeRamp {\\n  return ramp as AbstractSoundVolumeRamp;\\n}\\n\\n/** Minimal AudioV2 surface (avoids dual-module StaticSound vs global AbstractSound mismatch). */\\ninterface AudioV2SoundLike {\\n  play: () => void;\\n  stop: () => void;\\n  dispose: () => void;\\n  setVolume: (volume: number, ramp?: AbstractSoundVolumeRamp) => void;\\n  volume: number;\\n  activeInstancesCount: number;\\n}\\n\\nexport function fromAbstractSound(sound: AudioV2SoundLike): ManagedAudioSound {\\n  return {\\n    play: () => {\\n      sound.play();\\n    },\\n    stop: () => {\\n      sound.stop();\\n    },\\n    dispose: () => {\\n      sound.dispose();\\n    },\\n    setVolume: (volume: number, ramp?: Partial<AudioVolumeRampOptions> | null) => {\\n      sound.setVolume(volume, toEngineVolumeRamp(ramp ?? null));\\n    },\\n    getVolume: () => sound.volume,\\n    isActive: () => sound.activeInstancesCount > 0\\n  };\\n}\\n\",\"types/behaviors.ts\":\"// ============================================================================\\n// BEHAVIOR TYPE DEFINITIONS\\n// ============================================================================\\n\\n/**\\n * Discriminated union for check period configuration\\n * Controls how frequently trigger conditions are evaluated\\n */\\nexport type CheckPeriod =\\n  | { readonly type: 'everyFrame' }\\n  | { readonly type: 'interval'; Item / physics-object\\n// transforms on the wire are **pose-only**: `{ pos: [x,y,z], rot: [x,y,z,w] }`\\n// (Invariant P) — see MULTIPLAYER_SYNCH.md §5.2 and the `sampleMeshPose` /\\n// `applyPoseToMesh` helpers below. Scale is never replicated: every client spawns the\\n// mesh with identical local `scaling` from its config, and that static scale factor is\\n// the same on all clients (negative-sign flips for GLB re-orientation included).\\n// Euler angles appear only in asset/config authoring and local character control; they\\n// are NEVER read or written on item paths (Invariant E).\\n//\\n// Historical notes:\\n//  - Earlier revisions shipped a 16-float world matrix and decomposed on the receiver.\\n//    That was ~2× the bandwidth and forced an expensive decomposition on both ends;\\n//    it also reintroduced the negative-scale decomposition trap whenever the sender's\\n//    world matrix contained a mirrored scale (collectibles with `-x`, `-z` sign flips).\\n//    See MULTIPLAYER_SYNCH.md §5.2 for the rationale behind pose-only transport.\\n//  - Earlier revisions provided an `applyMatrixToBody` helper that drove\\n//    `PhysicsBody.setTargetTransform`. Per MULTIPLAYER_SYNCH.md §B.9 (mesh-direct\\n//    kinematic apply), replicas now always write the mesh transform and let Havok's\\n//    pre-step sync propagate to the body. `applyMatrixToBody` has been removed to\\n//    prevent regressions.\\n\\nimport type { CharacterController } from '../controllers/character_controller';\\nimport type { QuaternionSerializable, Vector3Serializable } from '../types/multiplayer';\\n\\nexport type { QuaternionSerializable } from '../types/multiplayer';\\n\\n/**\\n * Serializes BABYLON.Quaternion to [x, y, z, w]\\n * Standard order: x, y, z, w (not w, x, y, z)\\n */\\nfunction serializeQuaternion(q: BABYLON.Quaternion): QuaternionSerializable {\\n  return [q.x, q.y, q.z, q.w];\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Deserializes [x, y, z, w] back to BABYLON.Quaternion\\n */\\nexport function deserializeQuaternion(q: QuaternionSerializable): BABYLON.Quaternion {\\n  return new BABYLON.Quaternion(q[0], q[1], q[2], q[3]);\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Normalizes a quaternion to unit length\\n */\\nexport function normalizeQuaternion(q: QuaternionSerializable): QuaternionSerializable {\\n  const lengthSq = q[0] * q[0] + q[1] * q[1] + q[2] * q[2] + q[3] * q[3];\\n  if (lengthSq === 0) {\\n    return [0, 0, 0, 1]; // Identity quaternion\\n  }\\n  const length = Math.sqrt(lengthSq);\\n  return [q[0] / length, q[1] / length, q[2] / length, q[3] / length];\\n}\\n\\n// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n// Item / physics-object pose helpers (Invariants P and E)\\n// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n/** World-coord clamp bound mirrored from `multiplayer_wire_guards.MAX_ABS_WORLD_COORD`. */\\nconst POSE_COORD_CLAMP = 5e6;\\n\\nfunction clampPoseComponent(n: number): number {\\n  if (!Number.isFinite(n)) {\\n    return 0;\\n  }\\n  return Math.max(-POSE_COORD_CLAMP, Math.min(POSE_COORD_CLAMP, n));\\n}\\n\\n/** Wire pose — position + unit quaternion, no scale. See Invariant P in MULTIPLAYER_SYNCH.md §5.2. */\\nexport interface WirePose {\\n  readonly pos: Vector3Serializable;\\n  readonly rot: QuaternionSerializable;\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Sample a mesh's world pose as `{ pos, rot }` directly from its local channels\\n * (`mesh.position`, `mesh.rotationQuaternion`). This is the sole transform payload\\n * carried on the item wire (Invariant P).\\n *\\n * **Unparented-mesh assumption.** Every replicated item mesh is unparented at spawn\\n * (`CollectiblesManager.createCollectibleInstance` / `createPhysicsInstance` and\\n * environment physics objects add the mesh directly to the scene). For an unparented\\n * mesh, `mesh.position` equals its world position and `mesh.rotationQuaternion` is\\n * the world rotation factor before scale — exactly what we need. We therefore avoid\\n * the cost of `computeWorldMatrix` + `decompose` on every sample.\\n *\\n * **Scale is never sampled.** The mesh's local `scaling` (including any negative-sign\\n * flips authored at spawn, e.g. `-x, -z` for GLB re-orientation of collectibles) is\\n * a static configuration value — every client sets the same value at spawn from the\\n * same `environment.items[*].instances[*].scale`. Shipping it would be a waste of\\n * bandwidth and CPU, and it is exactly what used to trigger the negative-scale\\n * decomposition trap when we shipped a world matrix instead.\\n *\\n * Rotation is normalized on send so downstream consumers can assume unit quaternions\\n * without re-measuring length. If the mesh has no `rotationQuaternion` (rare — only\\n * if someone mutated `mesh.rotation` directly, which we forbid on item paths per\\n * Invariant E), we emit the identity rotation.\\n */\\nexport function sampleMeshPose(mesh: BABYLON.AbstractMesh): WirePose {\\n  const p = mesh.position;\\n  const q = mesh.rotationQuaternion;\\n  const rot: QuaternionSerializable = q ? normalizeQuaternion([q.x, q.y, q.z, q.w]) : [0, 0, 0, 1];\\n  return {\\n    pos: [clampPoseComponent(p.x), clampPoseComponent(p.y), clampPoseComponent(p.z)],\\n    rot\\n  };\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Write a wire `{ pos, rot }` pair onto a mesh's local channels (Invariant P). This is\\n * the canonical replica-apply primitive — see MULTIPLAYER_SYNCH.md §B.9 for why we do\\n * NOT drive `body.setTargetTransform` here.\\n *\\n * The sender and receiver both assume the mesh is unparented and has the same local\\n * `scaling` (set at spawn from the shared config). Writing `pos` and\\n * `rotationQuaternion` verbatim therefore reproduces the sender's world transform\\n * exactly, without any matrix decomposition on either end and without clobbering the\\n * local scale. This side-steps the negative-scale decomposition trap (see\\n * MULTIPLAYER_SYNCH.md §5.2) that the 16-float-matrix wire format was vulnerable to:\\n * the receiver's scale is its own authored value, and the rotation quaternion is the\\n * sender's *pre-scale* local rotation, so no mirrored-axis ambiguity ever arises.\\n *\\n * When the mesh has an associated ANIMATED physics body, Havok's pre-step sync copies\\n * these values to the body before the next physics tick so collision queries see the\\n * correct pose. The Euler `mesh.rotation` channel is never written (Invariant E).
